questions.question: It is the only mode of transport to reach the most remote and distant areas:
questions.explanation: The airway is the fastest way of transport developed in the early twentieth century. It is also the most expensive due to the high cost of fuel. Air traffic is adversely affected by bad weather like fog and storms. It is the only mode of transport to reach the most remote and distant areas especially where there are no roads and railways. Helicopters are extremely useful in most inaccessible areas and in times of calamities for rescuing people and distributing food, water, clothes, and medicines.

questions.question: Which Railway is the longest railway system in the World?
questions.explanation: The Trans-Siberian Railway is a network of railways connecting Moscow with the Russian Far East. With a length of 9,289 kilometres, it is the longest railway line in the world. There are connecting branch lines into Mongolia, China, and North Korea. It has connected Moscow with Vladivostok since 1916 and is still being expanded.

questions.question: Waterways are mainly of two types, these are:
questions.explanation: Since early days waterways were used for transportation. Waterways are the cheapest for carrying heavy and bulky goods over long distances. They are mainly of two types – inland waterways and sea routes.Sea routes and oceanic routes are mostly used for transporting merchandise and goods from one country to another. These routes are connected with the ports. Navigable rivers and lakes are used as inland waterways. Some of the important inland waterways are the Ganga-Brahmaputra river system.

questions.question: Airways is the fastest way of transport developed in the early ________.
questions.explanation: The airway is the most expensive way to reach our destination, due to the high cost of fuel. It is the fastest way of transport developed in the early twentieth century. Air traffic is adversely affected by bad weather like fog and storms. It is the only mode of transport to reach the most remote and distant areas especially where there are no roads and railways.

questions.question: ________ is most adversely affected by bad weather like fog and storms.
questions.explanation: The airway is the fastest way of transport developed in the early twentieth century. It is also the most expensive due to the high cost of fuel. Air traffic is adversely affected by bad weather like fog and storms. It is the only mode of transport to reach the most remote and distant areas especially where there are no roads and railways.

questions.question: Following are the natural conditions for selection of an ideal site except:
questions.explanation: Settlements are places where people build their homes. Early human beings lived on trees and in caves. When they started to grow crops it became necessary to have a permanent home. The place where a building or a settlement develops is called its site. The natural conditions for selection of an ideal site are- favourable climate, availability of water, suitable land, and fertile soil.

questions.question: Where did the early settlements grow?
questions.explanation: Settlements are places where people build their homes. Early human beings lived on trees and in caves. When they started to grow crops it became necessary to have a permanent home. The settlements grew near the river valleys as the water was available and the land was fertile. With the development of trade, commerce, and manufacturing, human settlements became larger. The settlement flourished and civilizations developed near river valleys, for example, Indus valley civilization.

questions.question: A scattered settlement is

options.html: a closely built area of dwellings, wherever flat land is available
a place where a building or a settlement develops
where dwellings are spaced over an extensive area
a place where people build their homes

questions.explanation: Settlements are places where people build their homes. Nowadays two different pictures of settlements have seen the rural and the urban settlements. Rural settlements can be compact or scattered. In a scattered settlement dwelling is spaced over an extensive area. This type of settlement is mostly found in hilly tracts, thick forests, and regions of extreme climate.

questions.question: The people living in deep forests, hot and cold deserts and mountains often dwell in a ________.
questions.explanation: Settlements are places where people build their homes. Settlements can be permanent or temporary. Settlements that are occupied for a short time are called temporary settlements. In a temporary settlement, people live in deep forests, hot and cold deserts, and mountains, they often dwell in such temporary settlements. They practice hunting, gathering, shifting cultivation, and transhumance.

questions.question: Mostly settlements now-a-days are ________.
questions.explanation: Settlements are places where people build their homes. Early human beings lived on trees and in caves. When they started to grow crops it became necessary to have a permanent home. Settlements that are occupied for a short time are called temporary settlements. However more and more settlements today are permanent settlements, In these settlements, people build homes to live in.

questions.question: In the village, you will found ________.
questions.explanation: In a scattered settlement dwelling is spaced over an extensive area. This type of settlement is mostly found in hilly tracts, thick forests, and regions of extreme climate. villages may not have big cinema halls, well-equipped schools, and good hospitals, but they have a lot of open spaces and fresh air to breathe in.

questions.question: A _____ settlement is a closely built area of dwellings.
questions.explanation: Two different settlements are - the rural and the urban settlements. Rural settlements can be compact or scattered. A compact settlement is a closely built area of dwellings, wherever flat land is available. In scattered settlement dwellings are spaced over an extensive area. This type of settlement is mostly found in hilly tracts, thick forests, and regions of extreme climate.

questions.question: The scattered settlement found in the following areas except:
questions.explanation: Two different settlements are the rural and urban settlements. Rural settlements can be compact or scattered. In a scattered settlement dwelling is spaced over an extensive area. This type of settlement is mostly found in hilly tracts, thick forests, and regions of extreme climate. In rural areas, people build houses to suit their environment.

questions.question: Thick mud-walled houses are very common in ________.
questions.explanation: In rural areas, people build houses to suit their environment. In regions of heavy rainfall, they have slanting roofs. Places where water accumulates in the rainy season the houses are constructed on a raised platform or stilts. Thick mud-walled houses with thatched roofs are very common in areas of a hot climate. Local materials like stones, mud, clay, straw, etc are used to construct houses.

questions.question: Yak animal for transport is used in ________.
questions.explanation: Transport is the means by which people and goods move. In the early days, it took a great deal of time, to travel long distances. People had to walk and used animals to carry their goods. With the passage of time different means of transport developed but even today people use animals for transport. In our country donkeys, mules, bullocks, and camels are common. In the hilly area of Tibet, the yaks are used as a means of transportation.

questions.question: The Golden Quadrilateral connects ________.

options.html: Uttar Pradesh, Mumbai, Chennai, and Kolkata

questions.explanation: The Golden Quadrilateral is a highway network connecting many of the major industrial, agricultural and cultural centres of India. A quadrilateral of sorts is formed by connecting Chennai, Kolkata, Delhi, and Mumbai. Some other metropolises are also connected by the network.

questions.question: ________ have a dense network of road.
questions.explanation: The most commonly used means of transport especially for short distances are roads. They can be metalled and unmetalled. The plains have a dense network of roads, as Expressways, and Highways. Roads have also been build in terrains like deserts, forests, and even high mountains, but the plains have more roads than the hilly area.

questions.question: Which highway in the Himalayan Mountains is one of the highest roadways in the world?
questions.explanation: Roads have been build in terrains like deserts, forests, and even high mountains. Manali-Leh highway in the Himalayan Mountains is one of the highest roadways in the world. The Leh-Manali Highway is a 490 km long highway in northernmost India connecting Leh in Ladakh in Jammu and Kashmir state and Manali in Himachal Pradesh state. The average elevation of Leh-Manali highway is more than 4,000 m and its highest elevation is 5,328 m at Tanglang La mountain pass.

questions.question: The train from Xining to Lhasa runs at an altitude of ________ above sea level.
questions.explanation: The train from Xining to Lhasa runs at an altitude of 4,000 m above sea level and the highest point is 5,072 m.

questions.question: The invention of the ________ helped in the speedy development of rail transport.
questions.explanation: The railways carry heavy goods and people over long distances quickly and cheaply. The invention of the steam engine and the Industrial Revolution helped in the speedy development of rail transport. Diesel and electric engines have largely replaced the steam engines. In places, super fast trains have been introduced to make the journey faster.

questions.question: Vladivostok is located on ________.
questions.explanation: Vladivostok is a city and the administrative center of Primorsky Krai, Russia, located around the Golden Horn Bay, not far from Russia's borders with China and North Korea. The city is the home port of the Russian Pacific Fleet and the largest Russian port on the Pacific Ocean. The Trans-Siberian Railway is the longest railway system connecting St. Petersburg in Western Russia to Vladivostok on the Pacific coast.

questions.question: The Trans-Siberian Railway connects:
questions.explanation: The Trans-Siberian Railway is a network of railways connecting Moscow with the Russian Far East. With a length of 9,289 km, It is the longest railway line in the world. It connects St. Petersburg in Western Russia to Vladivostok on the Pacific coast. There are other connecting branch lines into Mongolia, China, and North Korea.

questions.question: Some of the important inland waterways are as follows except:
questions.explanation: In early days waterways were used for transportation. Waterways are the cheapest for carrying heavy and bulky goods over long distances. They are mainly of two types - inland waterways and sea routes. Navigable rivers and lakes are used as inland waterways. Some of the important inland waterways are the Ganga-Brahmaputra river system, the Great Lakes in North America, and the river Nile in Africa.

questions.question: ________ are extremely useful in most inaccessible areas in a time of natural calamities.
questions.explanation: This is the fastest way of transport developed in the early twentieth century. It is also the most expensive due to the high cost of fuel. It is the only mode of transport to reach the most remote and distant areas especially where there are no roads and railways. Helicopters are extremely useful in most inaccessible areas and in time of calamities for rescuing people and distributing food, water, clothes, and medicines.

questions.question: Media with which a large number of people can communicate is called ________.
questions.explanation: Communication is the process of conveying messages to others. With the development of technology, humans have devised new and fast modes of communication. Different modes of communication are used to provide information, to educate as well as to entertain. Through newspapers, radio and television we can communicate with a large number of people. They are called mass media.

questions.question: ________ have helped in oil exploration, the survey of forest, underground water, mineral wealth, weather forecast, and disaster warning.
questions.explanation: The satellites have made communication even faster. Satellites have helped in oil exploration, the survey of forest, underground water, mineral wealth, weather forecast, and disaster warning. Now we can send electronic mails or e-mails through the Internet. Wireless telephonic communications through cellular phones have become very popular today.

questions.question: The railway network is well developed over the ________ area.

The railway network is well developed over the plain areas. Advanced technological skills have enabled laying of railway lines in difficult mountain terrains also.

questions.question: What is Transhumance?
questions.explanation: Transhumance is a seasonal movement of people. People who rear animals move in search of new pastures according to changes in seasons. They used to live in a temporary settlement.

questions.question: The main river along with all its tributaries that drain an area forms a river ________.
questions.explanation: Tributaries are small rivers that join the main river. The main river along with all its tributaries that drain an area form a river basin or the catchment area. The Amazon Basin is the largest river basin in the world. The place where a river flows into another body of water is called the river’s mouth.

questions.question: The Amazon Basin is the largest river basin in the ________.
questions.explanation: Numerous tributaries join the Amazon River to form the Amazon basin. The river basin drains portions of Brazil, parts of Peru, Bolivia, Ecuador, Columbia, and a small part of Venezuela. The main river along with all its tributaries that drain an area form a river basin or the catchment area. The Amazon Basin is the largest river basin in the world.

questions.question: Flora stands for:
questions.explanation: Derived from Latin, Flora means a plant or the flowers of spring. Flora is a Sabine-derived goddess of flowers and of the season of spring - a symbol for nature and flowers (especially the may-flower).

questions.question: The Amazon Basin is in ________.
questions.explanation: The main river along with all its tributaries that drain an area form a river basin or the catchment area. The Amazon Basin is the largest river basin in the world. Numerous tributaries join the Amazon River to form the Amazon basin. The river basin drains portions of Brazil, parts of Peru, Bolivia, Ecuador, Columbia, and a small part of Venezuela. The Amazon Basin is in South America.

questions.question: On The Amazon basin, days and nights are almost:
questions.explanation: The Amazon Basin stretches directly on the equator and is characterized by hot and wet climate throughout the year. Both day and nights are almost equally hot and humid. The skin feels sticky. It rains almost everyday, that too without much warning. The day temperatures are high with very high humidity. At night the temperature goes down but the humidity remains high.

questions.question: ________ are special plants that store water in their leaves.
questions.explanation: Rain forests are in fact so thick that the dense “roof” created by leaves and branches does not allow the sunlight to reach the ground. The ground remains dark and damp. Only shade-tolerant vegetation may grow here. Orchids, bromeliads grow as plant parasites in these forests. Bromeliads are special plants that store water in their leaves. Animals like frogs use these pockets of water for laying their eggs.

questions.question: Flash eating ________ fish is also found in the river of rainforest area of Amazon.
questions.explanation: The Amazon rainforest is rich in fauna. Birds such as toucans, humming-birds, birds of paradise with their brilliantly coloured plumage, are usually seen there. Various species of reptiles and snakes also thrive in these jungles. Crocodiles, snakes, pythons abound. Anaconda and boa constrictor are some of the species. Besides, the Amazon basin is home to thousands of species of insects. Several species of fishes including the flesh-eating Piranha fish is also found in the river.

questions.question: In the rainforest how the work is divided among the men and women?

options.html: Women make pottery and man hunt and fish along the rivers
Women hunt and fish along the rivers, men take care of the crops
Men hunt and fish along the rivers, women take care of the crops
Men do cultivation along the rivers, women take care of the crops

questions.explanation: People of the rain forest grow most of their food in small areas after clearing some trees in the forest. While men hunt and fish along the rivers, women take care of the crops. They mainly grow tapioca, pineapple, and sweet potato. As hunting and fishing are uncertain it is the women who keep their families alive by feeding them the vegetables they grow. They practice “slash and burn agriculture”.

questions.question: The staple food in the rainforest area is ________.
questions.explanation: People in the rainforest grow most of their food in small areas after clearing some trees in the forest. While men hunt and fish along the rivers, women take care of the crops. They mainly grow tapioca, pineapple, and sweet potato. They practice “slash and burn agriculture”. The staple food is manioc, also known as cassava that grows under the ground like the potato. They also eat queen ants and egg sacs. Cash crops like coffee, maize, and cocoa are also grown.

questions.question: ________ is a way of cultivating land where farmers clear a piece of land by slashing or cutting down trees and bushes.
questions.explanation: Slash and Burn is a way of cultivating land where farmers clear a piece of land by slashing or cutting down trees and bushes. These are then burnt, which releases the nutrients into the soil. Now crops are grown in this cleared field for a few years. After repeatedly using the patch of land, the soil loses its nutrients. So it is abandoned. Then they clear another plot of land to plant. In the meantime, young trees grow in the old field. In this way, soil fertility is restored. People can then return to it and start cultivating it again.

questions.question: Which of the following is not the cash crop grown by the people living in Rainforests?
questions.explanation: People of the Rainforest grow most of their food in small areas after clearing some trees in the forest. While men hunt and fish along the rivers, women take care of the crops. They mainly grow tapioca, pineapple, and sweet potato. They practice “slash and burn agriculture”. The staple food is manioc, also known as cassava that grows under the ground like the potato. They also eat queen ants and egg sacs. Cash crops like coffee, maize, and cocoa are also grown by them.

questions.question: The Taj Mahal is located in ________.
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is an ivory-white marble mausoleum on the south bank of the Yamuna river in the Indian city of Agra. It was commissioned in 1632 by the Mughal emperor, Shah Jahan.

questions.question: Buddhist Stupa is situated in which states on the Basin of Ganga-Brahmaputra river?
questions.explanation: Tourism is another important activity of the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin. Buddhist stupas in Uttar Pradesh and Bihar, Lucknow with its Imambara, Taj Mahal on the banks of River Yamuna in Agra, Assam with Kaziranga and Manas wildlife sanctuaries and Arunachal Pradesh with a distinct tribal culture are some of the places situated near this river basin.

questions.question: Following are the tributaries of the River Ganga except:
questions.explanation: The Ganges or Ganga is a trans-boundary river of Asia which flows through the nations of India and Bangladesh. The 2,525 km long river rises in the eastern Himalayas in the Indian state of Uttarakhand and flows south and east through the Gangetic Plain of North India into Bangladesh, where it empties into the Bay of Bengal. The main tributaries of the River Ganga are the Ghaghra, the Son, the Chambal, the Gandak, the Kosi.

questions.question: The Ganga-Brahmaputra basin is in ________.
questions.explanation: The tributaries of rivers Ganga and Brahmaputra together form the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin in the Indian subcontinent. The basin lies in the sub-tropical region that is situated between 10°N to 30°N latitudes. The tributaries of the River Ganga like the Ghaghra, the Son, the Chambal, the Gandak, the Kosi, and the tributaries of Brahmaputra drain it.

questions.question: ______ are built on steep slopes to create flat surfaces on which crops are grown.
questions.explanation: In the mountains and hills, where the slopes are gentle, crops are grown on terraces. In agriculture, a terrace is a piece of sloped plane that has been cut into a series of successively receding flat surfaces or platforms, which resemble steps, for the purposes of more effective farming. The slope is removed so that water does not run off rapidly.

questions.question: Piranha is a ________.
questions.explanation: The Amazon basin is home to thousands of species of insects. Several species of fishes including the flesh-eating Piranha fish is also found in the river. This basin is thus extraordinarily rich in the variety of life found there.

questions.question: The place where a river flows into another body of water is called its ________.
questions.explanation: The place where a river flows into another body of water is called the river’s mouth, A river mouth is the part of a river where the river flows into another river, a lake, a reservoir, a sea or an ocean.

questions.question: Which forests cover the Ganga-Brahmaputra delta regions?
questions.explanation: The tributaries of rivers Ganga and Brahmaputra together form the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin in the Indian subcontinent. The plains of the Ganga and the Brahmaputra, the mountains and the foothills of the Himalayas and the Sundarbans delta are the main features of this basin. The delta area is covered with mangrove forests.

questions.question: The Ganga-Brahmaputra basin area is dominated by monsoon climate. The monsoon brings rains from ________ to ________.
questions.explanation: The tributaries of rivers Ganga and Brahmaputra together form the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin in the Indian subcontinent. The plains of the Ganga and the Brahmaputra, the mountains and the foothills of the Himalayas and the Sundarbans delta are the main features of this basin. The area is dominated by a monsoon climate. The monsoon brings rain from mid-June to mid-September. The summers are hot and the winters cool in this basin.

questions.question: What is the main occupation of the people living in the area of Ganga-Brahmaputra basin?
questions.explanation: The tributaries of rivers Ganga and Brahmaputra together form the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin in the Indian subcontinent. The plain area of this basin provides the most suitable land for human habitation. The soil is fertile, Agriculture is the main occupation of the people where flat land is available to grow crops. The density of the population of the plains is very high. The main crop is paddy. Since the cultivation of paddy requires sufficient water, it is grown in the areas where the amount of rainfall is high.

questions.question: Monkey, sloth, and ant-eating tapus animals are found in ________.
questions.explanation: Birds such as toucans, humming-birds, bird of paradise with their brilliantly coloured plumage, oversized bills for eating make them different from birds we commonly see in India. Animals like monkeys, sloth and ant-eating tapirs are found in the Amazon basin, Various species of reptiles and snakes also thrive in these jungles. Crocodiles, snakes, pythons abound. Anaconda and boa constrictor are some of the species.

questions.question: Manas wildlife sanctuary is in which state?
questions.explanation: Manas National Park or Manas Wildlife Sanctuary is a national park, UNESCO Natural World Heritage site, an elephant reserve, and a biosphere reserve in Assam, India. The name of the park is originated from the Manas River, which is named after the serpent goddess Manasa. The Manas river is a major tributary of Brahmaputra River, which passes through the heart of the national park.

questions.question: What type of trees are found in the Ganga Plain?
questions.explanation: In the Ganga and Brahmaputra plain tropical deciduous trees grow, along with teak, sal, and peepal. Thick bamboo groves are common in the Brahmaputra plain. The delta area is covered with mangrove forests. In parts of Uttaranchal, Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh, coniferous trees like pine, deodar and fir can be seen because the climate is cool and the slopes are steep.

questions.question: An important port on river Hoogly is ________.
questions.explanation: The Port of Kolkata is a riverine port in the city of Kolkata, India, located around 203 kilometres from the sea. It is the oldest operating port in India and was constructed by the British East India Company. It is situated on the left bank of the Hooghly River at about 203 km upstream from the sea.

questions.question: The cash crop is grown in the area of Ganga-Brahmaputra basin are ________.
questions.explanation: Agriculture is the main occupation of the people where flat land is available to grow crops. The main crop is paddy. Since the cultivation of paddy requires sufficient water, it is grown in the areas where the amount of rainfall is high. Wheat, maize, sorghum, gram, and millets are the other crops that are grown. Cash crops like sugarcane and jute are also grown. Banana plantations are seen in some areas of the plain. In West Bengal and Assam tea is grown in the plantation.

questions.question: Climate found in the Amazon basin is ________.
questions.explanation: The Amazon Basin stretches directly on the equator and is characterized by a hot and wet climate throughout the year. Both day and nights are almost equally hot and humid. It rains almost every day, that too without much warning. The day temperatures are high with very high humidity. At night the temperature goes down but the humidity remains high.

questions.question: In which of the following area of Ganga-Brahmaputra basin tea is grown in plantations?
questions.explanation: The Ganga-Brahmaputra basin is in the Indian subcontinent. The basin lies in the sub-tropical region. Agriculture is the main occupation of the people. In West Bengal and Assam tea is grown in plantations.

questions.question: Following are the tropical deciduous trees grow in the area of Ganga-Brahmaputra basin except:
questions.explanation: In the Ganga and Brahmaputra plain tropical deciduous trees grow, along with teak, sal, and peepal. Thick bamboo groves are common in the Brahmaputra plain. The delta area is covered with mangrove forests. In parts of Uttaranchal, Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh, coniferous trees like pine, deodar and fir can be seen because the climate is cool and the slopes are steep.

questions.question: The one-horned rhinoceros is found in the ________.
questions.explanation: There is a variety of wildlife in the basin. Elephants, tigers, deer, and monkeys are common. The one-horned rhinoceros is found in the Brahmaputra plain. In the delta area, Bengal tiger, crocodiles, and alligators are found.

questions.question: In the fresh waters of River Ganga and River Brahmaputra, a variety of dolphin locally called ________ are found.
questions.explanation: In the fresh waters of River Ganga and River Brahmaputra, a variety of dolphin locally called Susu (also called blind dolphin) is found. The presence of Susu is an indication of the health of the river. The untreated industrial and urban wastes with a high amount of chemicals are killing this species.

questions.question: The Ganga-Brahmaputra plain has several big towns and cities except for _______.
questions.explanation: The tributaries of rivers Ganga and Brahmaputra together form the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin in the Indian subcontinent. The Ganga-Brahmaputra plain has several big towns and cities. The cities of Allahabad, Kanpur, Varanasi, Lucknow, Patna, and Kolkata all with a population of more than ten lakhs are located along the River Ganga. Kerala is a state situated in southern India.

questions.question: Paddy is grown in ________.
questions.explanation: In the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin agriculture is the main occupation of the people where flat land is available to grow crops. The main crop is paddy. Since the cultivation of paddy requires sufficient water, it is grown in the areas where the amount of rainfall is high. Wheat, maize, sorghum, gram, and millets are the other crops that are grown. Cash crops like sugarcane and jute are also grown. Banana plantations are seen in some areas of the plain. In West Bengal and Assam tea is grown in plantations.

questions.question: Kolkata is an important port on the River ________.
questions.explanation: All four ways of transport are well developed in the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin. In the plain areas, the roadways and railways transport the people from one place to another. The waterways are an effective means of transport, particularly along the rivers. Kolkata is an important port on the River Hooghly.

questions.question: Which of the following is false regarding the wildlife living in the Ganga-Brahmaputra plain?

options.html: In the deltaic area Bengal tiger, Crocodile, and Alligator are found
One-horned Rhinoceros found in these plain
The most popular variety of fish are Catla, Hilsa found in the Ganga river
Dog, Langoor, and Tiger are a common animal found in these areas

questions.explanation: There is a variety of wildlife in the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin. Elephants, tigers, deer, and monkeys are common. The one-horned rhinoceros is found in the Brahmaputra plain. In the delta area, Bengal tiger, crocodiles, and alligators are found. Aquatic life abounds in the fresh river waters, the lakes, and the Bay of Bengal Sea. The most popular varieties of fish are the Rohu, Catla, and Hilsa.

questions.question: Agra City is situated on the riverbank of ________.
questions.explanation: Agra city is situated on the banks of the river Yamuna in the northern state of Uttar Pradesh, India. Agra is a major tourist destination because of its many Mughal-era buildings, most notably the Taj Mahal, Agra Fort and Fatehpur Sikri, all three of which are UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

questions.question: ________ explorers discovered the Amazon River.
questions.explanation: When Spanish explorers discovered the Amazon river, they were attacked by a group of local tribes wearing headgears and grass skirts. These people reminded them of the fierce tribes of women warriors known in the ancient Roman Empire as the Amazons. Hence the name Amazon.

questions.question: These are small rivers that join the main river:
questions.explanation: Tributaries are the small rivers that join the main river. The main river along with all its tributaries that drain an area form a river basin or the catchment area. The Amazon Basin is the largest river basin in the world.

questions.question: Amazon Basin characterized by ________.
questions.explanation: The Amazon Basin stretches directly on the equator and is characterized by a hot and wet climate throughout the year. Both day and nights are almost equally hot and humid. It rains almost every day, that too without much warning. The day temperatures are high with very high humidity. At night the temperature goes down but the humidity remains high.

questions.question: Which of the following is false regarding the Amazon basin climate?

options.html: The night temperatures are low with very low humidity
It has a hot and wet climate throughout the year
It rains almost every day, that too without much warning
Both day and nights are almost equally hot and humid

questions.explanation: The Amazon Basin stretches directly on the equator and is characterized by hot and wet climate throughout the year. Both day and nights are almost equally hot and humid. It rains almost every day, that too without much warning. The day temperatures are high with very high humidity. At night the temperature goes down but the humidity remains high.

questions.question: Which plants grow in Amazon forests?
questions.explanation: As it rains heavily in the Amazon region, thick forests grow. The forests are in fact so thick that the dense “roof” created by leaves and branches does not allow the sunlight to reach the ground. The ground remains dark and damp. Only shade-tolerant vegetation may grow here. Orchids, bromeliads grow as plant parasites.

questions.question: Following are found in Amazon forest except ________.
questions.explanation: The rainforest or the Amazon forest is rich in fauna. Birds such as toucans, hummingbirds, birds of paradise with their brilliantly coloured plumage are generally found there.

questions.question: Crops mainly grown in the rainforest are as follows except:
questions.explanation: In the Rainforest people grow most of their food in small areas after clearing some trees in the forest. While men hunt and fish along the rivers, women take care of the crops. They mainly grow tapioca, pineapple, and sweet potato. As hunting and fishing are uncertain it is the women who keep their families alive by feeding them the vegetables they grow.

questions.question: Bromeliads are special plants that store water in their leaves. Which use these pockets of water for laying their eggs?
questions.explanation: Orchids, bromeliads grow as plant parasites in the Rainforest. Bromeliads are special plants that store water in their leaves. Animals like frogs use these pockets of water for laying their eggs.

questions.question: Animals found in Amazon forest are-
questions.explanation: The Amazon rainforest is rich in fauna. Birds such as toucans, hummingbirds, are usually seen there. Animals like monkeys, sloths, and ant-eating tapirs are found here. Various species of reptiles and snakes also thrive in these jungles, Crocodiles, snakes, pythons are also found. Besides, the basin is home to thousands of species of insects. Several species of fishes including Piranha fish is also found in the river.

questions.question: In ________ the Trans Amazon highway made all parts of the rainforest accessible.
questions.explanation: The life of the people of the Amazon basin is slowly changing. In the older days, the heart of the forest could be reached only by navigating the river. In 1970 the Trans Amazon highway made all parts of the rainforest accessible. Aircraft and helicopters are also used for reaching various places. The indigenous population was pushed out from the area and forced to settle in new areas where they continued to practice their distinctive way of farming.

questions.question: Which of the following is false regarding slash and burn cultivation?

options.html: It is a way of cultivating land where farmers clear a piece of land by slashing or cutting down trees and bushes.
Cultivations are done on the same land for a long period of time.
The land is then burnt, which releases the nutrients into the soil.
Crops are grown in this cleared field for a few years.

questions.explanation: Slash and Burn is a way of cultivating land where farmers clear a piece of land by slashing or cutting down trees and bushes. These are then burnt, which releases the nutrients into the soil. Now crops are grown in this cleared field for a few years. After repeatedly using the patch of land, the soil looses its nutrients. So it is abandoned. Then they clear another plot of land to plant. In the meantime, young trees grow in the old field. In this way, soil fertility is restored. People can then return to it and start cultivating it again

questions.question: Tributaries of the river Brahmaputra are,except ________.
questions.explanation: Son River of central India is the second-largest tributary of the river Ganga after Yamuna River.

questions.question: The Ganga-Brahmaputra basin lies in the sub-tropical region that is situated between ________.
questions.explanation: The tributaries of rivers Ganga and Brahmaputra together form the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin in the Indian subcontinent. The basin lies in the sub-tropical region that is situated between 10oN to 30oN latitudes.

questions.question: ________ means the number of persons that live in one sq. km. of area.
questions.explanation: Population density means the number of persons that live in one sq. km. of area e.g. the population density of Uttarakhand is 159 while the density of West Bengal is 904 and that of Bihar is 880.

questions.question: Rainforest is depleting because of the following reason except:

options.html: People plant more and more tree
Big development activities result in the cutting of trees
Setting up of large scale industries by clearing forest

questions.explanation: Planting trees is one of the remedies.

questions.question: Delta in Ganga-Brahmaputra basin is ________.
questions.explanation: The tributaries of rivers Ganga and Brahmaputra together form the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin in the Indian subcontinent. The plains of the Ganga and the Brahmaputra, the mountains and the foothills of the Himalayas and the Sundarbans delta are the main features of this basin.

questions.question: What is the main crop grown in the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin area?
questions.explanation: In the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin, The soil is fertile. Agriculture is the main occupation of the people where flat land is available to grow crops. The density of the population of the plains is very high. The main crop is paddy. Since the cultivation of paddy requires sufficient water, it is grown in the areas where the amount of rainfall is high. Wheat, maize, sorghum, gram, and millets are the other crops that are grown. Cash crops like sugarcane and jute are also grown.

questions.question: ________ is produced in parts of Bihar and Assam.
questions.explanation: In the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin. The soil is fertile. Agriculture is the main occupation of the people where flat land is available to grow crops. The density of the population of the plains is very high. The main crop is paddy. Wheat, maize, sorghum, gram, and millets are the other crops that are grown. Cash crops like sugarcane and jute are also grown. Banana plantations are seen in some areas of the plain. Silk is produced through the cultivation of silkworms in parts of Bihar and Assam.

questions.question: Following are the statements regarding the vegetation coverage in the Ganga and Brahmaputra plain except for:

options.html: Sugarcane and jute cash crop are grown here
Wheat, maize, gram, millet are the crop grown here

questions.explanation: In the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin, the soil is fertile. Agriculture is the main occupation of the people where flat land is available to grow crops. The main crop is paddy. Wheat, maize, sorghum, gram, and millets are the other crops that are grown. Cash crops like sugarcane and jute are also grown. Banana plantations are seen in some areas of the plain. In West Bengal and Assam tea is grown in plantations. Silk is produced through the cultivation of silkworms in parts of Bihar and Assam.

questions.question: In the Ganga and Brahmaputra plain ________ trees grow.
questions.explanation: In the Ganga and Brahmaputra plain tropical deciduous trees grow, along with teak, sal, and peepal. Thick bamboo groves are common in the Brahmaputra plain. The delta area is covered with mangrove forests. In parts of Uttaranchal, Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh, coniferous trees like pine, deodar and fir can be seen because the climate is cool and the slopes are steep.

questions.question: Which of the following are coniferous trees?
questions.explanation: In parts of Uttaranchal, Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh, coniferous trees like pine, deodar and fir can be seen because the climate is cool and the slopes are steep.

questions.question: In parts of Uttarakhand, Sikkim, and Arunachal Pradesh which type of trees are found?
questions.explanation: In the Ganga and Brahmaputra plain tropical deciduous trees grow, along with teak, sal, and peepal. Thick bamboo groves are common in the Brahmaputra plain. The delta area is covered with mangrove forests. In parts of Uttaranchal, Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh, coniferous trees like pine, deodar and fir can be seen because the climate is cool and the slopes are steep.

questions.question: The most popular varieties of the fish are as follows except ________.
questions.explanation: There is a variety of wildlife in the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin. Elephants, tigers, deer, and monkeys are common. The one-horned rhinoceros is found in the Brahmaputra plain. In the delta area, Bengal tiger, crocodiles, and alligators are found. Aquatic life abounds in the fresh river waters, the lakes, and the Bay of Bengal Sea. The most popular varieties of fish are the Rohu, Catla, and Hilsa.

questions.question: Identify from where these people belong.

options.html: From various part of the world

questions.explanation: “In this picture, you can see people from different parts of the world – some from dry deserts, some from frozen lands and some from hot wet rainforests. “They may look different, but they share the same basic needs of life – food, clothing, and shelter”.

questions.question:
Match the following:

(a) Susu (i) Blind dolphins
(b) Tsangpo (ii) Original occupants of the region
(c) Indigenous (iii) Brahmaputra river name in Tibet
questions.explanation:
1. In the fresh waters of River Ganga and River Brahmaputra, a variety of dolphin locally called Susu (also called blind dolphin) is found. The presence of Susu is an indication of the health of the river.
  2. The Brahmaputra is one of the major rivers of Asia, a trans-boundary river which flows through China, India, and Bangladesh. It is also called Tsangpo-Brahmaputra when referring to the whole river including the stretch within Tibet. The Manas River, which runs through Bhutan, joins it at Jogighopa, in India. It is the tenth-largest river in the world by discharge and the 15th longest.
  3. Original occupants of the region or the native of a particular region.

questions.question: Identify the food, it is a staple food of rainforest area of Amazon.
questions.explanation: People grow most of their food in small areas after clearing some trees in the forest. While men hunt and fish along the rivers, women take care of the crops. They mainly grow pineapple and sweet potato. They practice “slash and burn agriculture”. The staple food is manioc, also known as cassava that grows under the ground like the potato. They also eat queen ants and egg sacs. Cash crops like coffee, maize and cocoa are also grown.

questions.question: Which of the following is false regarding the Amazon forest?

options.html: The rainforest is rich in fauna
It rains heavily in this region, thin forests grow
Only shade-tolerant vegetation may grow here
The forests are in fact so thick that the dense roof is created by leaves

questions.explanation: The Amazon Basin stretches directly on the equator and is characterized by a hot and wet climate throughout the year. Both day and nights are almost equally hot and humid. The skin feels sticky. It rains almost every day, that too without much warning. As it rains heavily in this region, thick forests grow. The forests are in fact so thick that the dense “roof” created by leaves and branches does not allow the sunlight to reach the ground.

questions.question:
Match the following:

(a) Summer of Ganga-Brahmaputra basin (i) Has inhospitable terrain
(b) The main occupation of people living in the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin (ii) Agriculture
(c) Mountain areas with steep slopes in the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin (iii) Hot
questions.explanation:
1. The plains of the Ganga and the Brahmaputra, the mountains and the foothills of the Himalayas and the Sundarbans delta are the main features of this basin. The area is dominated by a monsoon climate. The monsoon brings rains from mid-June to mid-September. The summers are hot and the winters cool.
  2. The basin area has a varied topography. The mountain areas with steep slopes have inhospitable terrain. Therefore less number of people live in the mountain area of the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin. The soil is fertile. Agriculture is the main occupation of the people where flat land is available to grow crops.
  3. The basin area has a varied topography. The environment plays a dominant role in the distribution of the population. The mountain areas with steep slopes have inhospitable terrain.

questions.question: Identify the bird shown on the below figure.
questions.explanation: The rainforest is rich in fauna. Birds such as toucans humming birds, bird of paradise with their brilliantly coloured plumage, are commonly seen there.

questions.question: In Amazon basin, the rain forests are so thick that the dense roof created by leaves and branches does not allow the ________ to reach the ground.
questions.explanation: As it rains heavily in the Amazon region, thick forests grow. The forests are in fact so thick that the dense “roof” created by leaves and branches does not allow the sunlight to reach the ground. The ground remains dark and damp.

questions.question:
Match the following:

(a) Delta area in Ganga-Brahmaputra basin (i) Tropical Deciduous
(b) Trees in Ganga-Brahmaputra basin (ii) Mangrove forest
(c) Forests in the Ganga-Plain (iii) Coniferous forest
questions.explanation: The vegetation cover of the area varies according to the type of landforms. In the Ganga and Brahmaputra plain tropical deciduous trees grow, along with teak, sal, and peepal. Thick bamboo groves are common in the Brahmaputra plain. The delta area is covered with mangrove forests. In parts of Uttaranchal, Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh, coniferous trees like pine, deodar and fir can be seen.

questions.question: Identify the animal.
questions.explanation: The rainforest is rich in fauna. Birds such as toucans, humming birds are seen there. These birds also make loud sounds in the forests. Animals like monkeys, sloth and ant-eating tapirs are found here. A tapir is a large, herbivorous mammal, similar in shape to a pig, with a short, prehensile nose trunk. Tapirs inhabit jungle and forest regions of South America, Central America, and Southeastern Asia.

questions.question:
Match the following:

(a) Manioc (i) Fish that eat fish
(b) Maloca (ii) The staple food of the people of Amazon basin
(c) Piranha (iii) House with slanting roof
questions.explanation:
1. In the Amazon basin, the staple food is manioc.
  2. There are large apartment-like houses called “Maloca” with a steeply slanting roof in the Amazon basin.
  3. A piranha is a fish with famously sharp teeth. Piranha eats other fishes.

questions.question: Identify the type of house.
questions.explanation: The rainforests of Amazon basin provide a lot of wood for the houses. Some families live in thatched houses shaped like beehives. There are other large apartment-like houses called “Maloca” with a steeply slanting roof. A Maloca is an ancestral long house used by the natives of the Amazon, notably in Colombia and Brazil. Each community has a maloca with its own unique characteristics.

questions.question: Identify which issue is shown in the below figure.
questions.explanation: The developmental activities are leading to the gradual destruction of the biologically diverse rainforests. It is estimated that a large area of the rainforest has been disappearing annually in the Amazon basin. You can see that this destruction of forests has a much wider implication. The topsoil is washed away as the rains fall and the lush forest turns into a barren landscape.

questions.question:
Brahmaputra river is known by a different name in different countries match them:

Name of Brahmaputra river Country/State
(a) Tsangpo (i) Bangladesh
(b) Jamuna (ii) China
(c) Dihang (iii) Assam
questions.explanation:
1. The Brahmaputra is called Tsangpo in Tibet.
  2. The Jamuna River is one of the three main rivers of Bangladesh. It is the main distributary channel of the Brahmaputra River as it flows from India to Bangladesh
  3. Dihang is another name for the river Brahmaputra.

questions.question: Identify what is shown in the given figure.
questions.explanation: In West Bengal and Assam tea is grown in plantations, Tea Gardens in Assam famed worldwide for the captive taste of its tea, Assam is world's largest tea-plantation area. The tea gardens add further charm toAssam's already picturesque panorama.

questions.question: Identify the crop which is shown in the below figure, it is the main crop of Ganga-Brahmaputra basin area
questions.explanation: The tributaries of rivers Ganga and Brahmaputra together form the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin in the Indian subcontinent. The plain area provides the most suitable land for human habitation. The soil is fertile. Agriculture is the main occupation of the people where flat land is available to grow crops. The density of population of the plains is very high. The main crop is paddy since cultivation of paddy requires sufficient water, it is grown in the areas where the amount of rainfall is high.

questions.question:
Match the following:

(a) Uttar Pradesh and Bihar (i) Imambara
(b) Assam (ii) Buddhist Stupa
(c) Lucknow (iii) Kaziranga
questions.explanation: Tourism is another important activity of the Ganga Bhrmaputra basin. Taj Mahal on the banks of River Yamuna in Agra, Allahabad on the confluence of the Rivers Ganga and Yamuna, Buddhists stupas in Uttar Pradesh and Bihar, Lucknow with its Imambara, Assam with Kaziranga and Manas with wildlife sanctuaries and Arunachal Pradesh with a distinct tribal culture are some of the places of this basin.

questions.question: It is an arid region characterised by extremely high or low temperatures and has scarce vegetation.
questions.explanation: Desert is a large, dry, barren region, usually having sandy or rocky soil and little or no vegetation.

questions.question: Sahara desert found in which continent?
questions.explanation: The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert and covers most of Northern Africa.

questions.question: Which of the following is the world's largest desert?
questions.explanation: The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert and covers most of Northern Africa.

questions.question: The Sahara desert touches ________ countries.
questions.explanation: The desert covers 11 countries. The desert encompasses, at least in part, the countries of Algeria, Chad, Egypt, Libya, Mali, Mauritania, Morocco, Niger, Western Sahara, Sudan, and Tunisia.

questions.question: Sahara rocky surface are ________ high.
questions.explanation: Sahara desert is covered with gravel plains and elevated plateaus with a bare rocky surface. These rocky surfaces may be more than 2500 m high at some places.

questions.question: Which part of the sheep and goat is used to make woollen clothes?
questions.explanation: Hair of sheep and goats are used for: mats, carpets, woollen clothes, and blankets.

questions.question: Sahara once used to be a ________.
questions.explanation: Sahara once used to be a lush green plain. Cave paintings in the Sahara desert show that there used to be rivers.

questions.question: Al Azizia is in ________.
questions.explanation: Al Azizia is in Sahara desert, south of Tripoli, Libya.

questions.question: Following animals found in the Sahara Desert except for ________.
questions.explanation: Camels, hyenas, jackals, foxes, scorpions, many varieties of snakes and lizards are the prominent animal species living in the Sahara desert.

questions.question: Domingo Paes was a traveller from which country?
questions.explanation: Portuguese traveller, Domingo Paes described Hampi in the sixteenth century.

questions.question: Following crops are grown in the Sahara Desert:
questions.explanation: Since water is available, the people grow date palms. Crops such as rice, wheat, barley, and beans are also grown.

questions.question: Tafilalet Oasis is in ________.
questions.explanation: Tafilalet Oasis in Morocco is a large oasis with an area of about 13,000 sq.km.

questions.question: Shey in Ladakh is famous for ________.
questions.explanation: The people here are either Muslims or Buddhists. In fact, several Buddhist monasteries dot the Ladakhi landscape with their traditional ‘gompas’. Some famous monasteries are Hemis, Thiksey, Shey, and Lamayuru.

questions.question: In Ladakh the word La means ________.
questions.explanation: Ladakh is made up of two words - La meaning mountain pass and Dak meaning country.

questions.question: Ladakh is a ________.
questions.explanation: Ladakh is a cold desert lying in the Great Himalayas, on the eastern side of Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.question: The Karakoram Range in the ________ and the Zanskar mountains in the ________ enclose Ladakh.
questions.explanation: The Karakoram Range in the north and the Zanskar mountains in the south enclose Ladhak.

questions.question: ________, one of the coldest inhabited places on earth is located in Ladakh.
questions.explanation: Drass, one of the coldest inhabited places on earth is located in Ladakh.

questions.question: The capital of Ladakh is:
questions.explanation: Leh is the capital of Ladakh, a region in the state of Jammu and Kashmir in India.

questions.question: Which of the following river flow in Ladakh?
questions.explanation: Leh is the capital of Ladakh and the largest town of the region. It is located to the north of the Indus River.

questions.question: Ladakh lies in the rain shadow of ________.
questions.explanation: As Ladhak in the rain shadow of the Himalayas, there is little rainfall, as low as 10 cm every year.

questions.question: The altitude in Ladakh varies from about ________.
questions.explanation: The altitude in Ladakh varies from about 3,000m in Kargil to more than 8,000m in the Karakoram.

questions.question: Due to the high altitude, the climate of Ladakh is ________.
questions.explanation: Due to its high altitude, the climate is extremely cold and dry.

questions.question: In Ladakh, it is freezing cold in the winters when the temperatures may remain below ________ for most of the time.
questions.explanation: The day temperatures in summer are just above zero degrees and the night temperatures well below 30oC. It is freezing cold in the winters when the temperatures may remain below 40oC for most of the time.

questions.question: There is little rainfall in the Ladakh as low as ________ every year.
questions.explanation: As Ladakh lies in the rain shadow of the Himalayas, there is little rainfall, as low as 10 cm every year.

questions.question: Manali - Leh highway crosses how many passes?
questions.explanation: Manali - Leh highway crosses four passes, Rohtang la, Baralacha la, Lungalacha la and Tanglang la.

questions.question: The oasis in the desert support ________ population.
questions.explanation: The oasis in the Sahara and the Nile Valley in Egypt supports the settled population.

questions.question: Ladakh is also known as ________.
questions.explanation: Ladakh is also known as Khapa-chan which means snow land.

questions.question: The Chiru or the Tibetan antelope is an ________.
questions.explanation: The Chiru or the Tibetan antelope is an endangered species. It is hunted for its wool known as shahtoosh.

questions.question: Shahtoosh is the name of ________.
questions.explanation: Shahtoosh is wool obtained from the Chiru or the Tibetan antelope.

questions.question: In Ladakh, ________ milk is used to make cheese and butter.
questions.explanation: In Ladhak the animals are reared to provide for the milk, meat, and hides. Yak’s milk is used to make cheese and butter.

questions.question: In Ladhak during the summers which fruit trees blooms?
questions.explanation: During the summers, fruit trees such as apples, apricots and walnuts bloom in Ladhak

questions.question: The finest cricket bats are made from the wood of the ________.
questions.explanation: Groves of willows are seen in the Ladhak valleys. The finest cricket bats are made from the wood of the willow trees.

questions.question: Which religious people live in Ladakh?
questions.explanation: The people here are either Muslims or Buddhists. In fact, several Buddhist monasteries are also found in Ladhak.

questions.question: Following are the famous monasteries of Buddhist except:
questions.explanation: Several Buddhist monasteries dot the Ladakhi landscape with their traditional ‘gompas’. Some famous monasteries are Hemis, Thiksey, Shey, and Lamayuru.

questions.question: Which National Highway connects Leh to Kashmir?
questions.explanation: The National Highway 1A connects Leh to Kashmir Valley through the Zoji la Pas.

questions.question: The Manali-Leh highway opens only between ________ when snow is cleared from the road.
questions.explanation: The highway opens only between July and September when snow is cleared from the road.

questions.question: Sahara desert has an area of around ________.
questions.explanation: It is the world’s largest desert. It has an area of around 8.54 million sq. km.

questions.question: India has an area of ________.
questions.explanation: It is the seventh-largest country in the world, with a total area of 3,287,263 square kilometres.

questions.question: Following countries touches the boundaries of the Sahara desert except:
questions.explanation: The Sahara desert touches eleven countries. These are Algeria, Chad, Egypt, Libya, Mali, Mauritania, Morocco, Niger, Sudan, Tunisia, and Western Sahara.

questions.question: Sahara desert is covered with ________.
questions.explanation: Besides the vast stretches of sands, that Sahara desert is covered with, there are also gravel plains and elevated plateaus with a bare rocky surface. These rocky surfaces may be more than 2500m high in some places.

questions.question: ________ in Sahara desert show that there used to be rivers with crocodiles. Elephants, lions, sheep, cattle, and goats were common animals.
questions.explanation: Cave paintings in the Sahara desert show that there used to be rivers with crocodiles. Elephants, lions, giraffes, ostriches, sheep, cattle, and goats were common animals.

questions.question: Sahara once used to be a lush green plain but changed in a climate-changed into ________ region.
questions.explanation: Present-day Sahara once used to be a lush green plain. Cave paintings in the Sahara desert show that there used to be a river. But the climate has changed it to a very hot and dry region.

questions.question: ________ recorded the highest temperature of 57.7oC.
questions.explanation: Al Azizia in Sahara desert, south of Tripoli, Libya recorded the highest temperature of 57.7oC in 1922.

questions.question: In the Sahara Desert, the temperatures during the day may soar as high as ________, heating up the sand and the bare rocks.
questions.explanation: Days are unbelievably hot. The temperatures during the day may soar as high as 50oC, heating up the sand and the bare rocks, which in turn radiates heat making everything around hot.

questions.question: In the Sahara Desert, the nights maybe:

options.html: as high as 50<sup>o</sup>C, heating up the sand and the bare rocks
as high as 0<sup>o</sup>C, heating up the sand and the bare rocks

questions.explanation: The climate of the Sahara desert during nights may be freezing cold with temperatures nearing zero degrees.

questions.question: Vegetation in the Sahara desert includes:
questions.explanation: Vegetation in the Sahara desert includes cactus, date palms, and acacia. In some places, there are oasis - green islands with date palms surrounding them.

questions.question: Scientists have actually found skeletons of ________ in Sahara desert.
questions.explanation: Scientists have actually found skeletons of fish in the Sahara desert.

questions.question: The fort at Masulipatnam was built by whom?
questions.explanation: The fort at Masulipatnam was built by the Dutch. The town of Masulipatnam or Machlipatnam (literally, fish port town) lay on the delta of the Krishna river.

questions.question: Tafilalet Oasis is a large oasis with an area of about ________.
questions.explanation: Tafilalet Oasis in Morocco is a large oasis with an area of about 13,000 sq.km.

questions.question: The discovery of oil a product in great demand throughout the world, in ________ is constantly transforming the Sahara desert.
questions.explanation: The discovery of oil - a product in great demand throughout the world, in Algeria, Libya, and Egypt is constantly transforming the Sahara desert.

questions.question:
Which of the following minerals found in the Sahara desert:

  1. Iron
  2. Phosphorus
  3. Aluminium
  4. Manganese
  5. Potassium
  6. Uranium
  7. Zinc
questions.explanation: Minerals of importance that are found in the Sahara area include iron, phosphorus, manganese, and uranium.

questions.question: ________ are seen acting as guides to foreign tourists in the Sahara Desert.
questions.explanation: Tuaregs are seen acting as guides to foreign tourists.

questions.question: In Ladakh the word dakh means:
questions.explanation: Ladakh is made up of two words - La meaning mountain pass and Dak meaning country.

questions.question: Ladakh is a cold desert lying in the Great Himalayas

options.html: on the eastern side of Himachal Pradesh
on the western side of Himachal Pradesh
on the western side of Jammu and Kashmir
on the eastern side of Jammu and Kashmir

questions.explanation: Ladakh is a cold desert lying in the Great Himalayas, on the eastern side of Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.question: Which glacier is found in Ladakh?
questions.explanation: Several glaciers are found in Ladakh, for example, the Gangari glacier.

questions.question: The altitude in Ladakh is 8,000m in the ________.
questions.explanation: The altitude in Ladakh varies from about 3,000m in Kargil to more than 8,000m in the Karakoram.

questions.question: During the summer season in Ladhak, the people are busy cultivating ________.

options.html: barley, tomato, cabbage, ladyfinger, and turnip
barley, potatoes, peas, beans, and turnip
barley, tomato, peas, beans, and turnip
barley, tomato, brinjal, beans, and turnip

questions.explanation: In the summer season, the people are busy cultivating barley, potatoes, peas, beans, and turnip.

questions.question: In Ladakh the day temperatures in summer are just above ________ and the night temperatures well below ________.
questions.explanation: The day temperatures in summer are just above 30°C and the night temperatures well below 0°C.

questions.question: Khapa-chan means ________.
questions.explanation: Ladakh is also known as Khapa-chan which means snow land.

questions.question: Chiru is hunted for its ________.
questions.explanation: The Chiru or the Tibetan antelope is an endangered species. It is hunted for its wool known as shahtoosh.

questions.question: Manali - Leh highway crosses four passes except ________.

questions.question: What was an important festival celebrated at Hampi?
questions.explanation: The Mahanavami festival, known today as Navaratri in the south, was one of the most important festivals celebrated at Hampi.

It has a short rainy season.

questions.question: India is a_______country.
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India guarantees equality to all citizens, it means that India is a democratic country.Ademocratic governmentinclude people’s participation, the resolution of conflict, equality and justice. Equality is a key feature of democracy and influences all aspects of its functioning.

questions.question: Every adult in a country, irrespective of their wealth and the communities she/he belongs to, has one vote that is-
questions.explanation: In a democratic country, like India, all adults irrespective of what religion they belong to, how much education they have had, what caste they are, or whether they are rich or poor are allowed to vote. This is called universal adult franchise and is an universal adult franchise essential aspect of all democracies. The idea of universal adult franchise is based on the idea of equality because it states that every adult in a country, irrespective of their wealth and the communities she/he belongs to, has one vote.

questions.question: One of the more common forms of inequality in India is the
questions.explanation: Apart from being poor, people in India experience inequality in different ways. Based on real incidents in people’s lives that reflects the different kinds of inequalities that exist in India. One of the more common forms of inequality in India is the caste system. If you live in rural India your caste identity is something that you probably learned or experienced very young. If you live in urban India some of you might think that people no longer believe in caste. But just look at the matrimonials shown from a leading English newspaper and you will see how important the issue of caste continues to be in the minds of highly educated urban Indians.

questions.question: Dalit means
questions.explanation: Dalit is a term that the so-called lower castes use to address themselves. Dalit means ‘broken’ and by using this word, lower castes are pointing to how they were, and continue to be, seriously discriminated.

questions.question: Which of the following is the autobiography of Omprakash Valmiki?
questions.explanation: Omprakash Valmiki is a famous Dalit writer. In his autobiography, Joothan, he writes, about the pain of being a dalit child as “I had to sit away from the others in the class, and that too on the floor. The mat ran out before reaching the spot I sat on. Sometimes I would have to sit way behind everybody, right near the door, sometimes they would beat me without any reason. The headmaster asked me to sweep the school and the playground. The other children in my class were studying and I was sweeping.

questions.question: The Ansari's dignity was also hurt when ____________ lease their apartment to them.
questions.explanation: The Ansaris and Omprakash Valmiki are being treated unequally on the basis of differences of caste and religion. When persons are treated unequally, their dignity is violated. The dignity of both Omprakash Valmiki and the Ansaris was violated because of the way in which they were treated. The Ansaris’ dignity was also hurt when persons refused to lease their apartments to them. However, when the property dealer suggested that they change their name, it was their dignity or self-respect that made them refuse this suggestion.

questions.question: Every person has access to all public places except
questions.explanation: Parliament is not means for public utility

questions.question: Which state was the first state in India to introduce mid day meal scheme and in 2001?
questions.explanation: The midday meal schem refers to the programme introduced in all government elementary schools to provide children with cooked lunch. Tamil Nadu was the first state in India to introduce this scheme, and in 2001, the Supreme Court asked all state governments to begin this programme in their schools within six months. This programme has had many positive effects. These include the fact that more poor children have begun enrolling and regularly attending school, their attendance has improved.

questions.question: In the United States of America, who faced discrimination?
questions.explanation: In many democratic countries around the world, the issue of equality continues to be the key issue around which communities struggle. In the United States of America, the African–Americans whose ancestors were the slaves who were brought over from Africa, continue to describe their lives today as largely unequal. This, despite the fact that there was a movement in the late 1950s to push for equal rights for African– Americans. Prior to this, African–Americans were treated extremely unequally in the United States and denied equality through law.

questions.question: Who was Rosa Parks?
questions.explanation: Rosa Parks was an African–American woman. Whose support started a huge agitation against the unequal ways in which African–Americans were treated in 1 december 1955.Which later came to be known as the Civil Rights Movement. The Civil Rights Movement Rights Act of 1964 prohibited discrimination on the basis of race, religion or national origin.

questions.question: Which Article of Indian Constitution relates to Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth?
questions.explanation: Article 15 of the constitution states that no person shall be discriminated on the basis of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth. Every person shall have equal access to public places like public parks, museums, wells, bathing ghats and temples etc. However, the State may make any special provision for women and children. Special provisions may be made for the advancements of any socially or educationally backward class orscheduled castesorscheduled tribes.

questions.question: Who is the most advantaged group of people
questions.explanation: Rich businessmen are the most privileged  group of people.

questions.question: Right to vote in India is known as--
questions.explanation: In a democratic country, like India, all adults irrespective of what religion they belong to, how much education they have had, what caste they are, or whether they are rich or poor are allowed to vote. This, is called universal adult franchise and it is an essential aspect of all democracies. The idea of universal adult franchise is based on the idea of equality because it states that every adult in a country, irrespective of their wealth and the communities she/he belongs to, has one vote.

questions.question: African-Americans were discriminated in-
questions.explanation: In many democratic countries around the world, the issue of equality continues to be the key issue around which communities struggle. So, for example, in the United States of America, the African–Americans whose ancestors were the slaves who were brought over from Africa, continue to describe their lives today as largely unequal. This, despite the fact that there was a movement in the late 1950s to push for equal rights for African– Americans. Prior to this, African–Americans were treated extremely unequal in the United States and denied equality through law.

questions.question: Tawa river is a tributory of Narmada river, It flows in?
questions.explanation: Tawa river is the Narmada's longest tributary, at 172 km. It rises in the Satpura Range of Betul and flowing north and west, joins the Narmada at the village of Bandra Bhan in Hoshangabad District of Madhya pradesh. In 1958, construction began on Tawa Dam, which was completed in 1978 in Tawa Nagar to create Tawa Reservoir in southern Hoshangabad District. Forty-four villages were submerged by the reservoir.

questions.question: Which of the following forest/hills lies in Madhya Pradesh?
questions.explanation: The Satpura Range is a range of hills in central India. The range rises in eastern Gujarat state running east through the border of Maharashtra and Madhya Pradesh to the east till Chhattisgarh. The range parallels the Vindhya Range to the north, and these two east-west ranges divide Indian Subcontinent into the Indo-Gangetic plain of northern India and the Deccan Plateau of the south. The Narmada River originates from north-eastern end of Satpura and runs in the depression between the Satpura and Vindhya ranges, draining the northern slope of the Satpura range.

questions.question: Tehri dam is situated in which state?
questions.explanation: A dam is built across a river at sites where one can collect a lot of water. This forms a reservoir and as the water collects it submerges vast areas of land.The Tehri Dam is the Highest dam in India and one of the highest in the world. It is a multi-purpose rock and earth-fill embankment dam on the Bhagirathi River near Tehri in Uttarakhand, India. It is the primary dam of the THDC India Ltd. and the Tehri hydroelectric complex.

questions.question: When Tehri dam was built in Uttarakahand nearly how many people were displaced?
questions.explanation: A dam is built across a river at sites where one can collect a lot of water. This forms a reservoir and as the water collects it submerges vast areas of land. This is because the wall of the dam across the river is high and the water spreads over a large area. The old Tehri town and 100 villages, some totally and some partially, were submerged by this dam. Nearly one lakh people were displaced.

questions.question: In ____ , the Madhya Pradesh government decided to give to the people displaced by the Tawa dam the fishing rights for the reservoir.
questions.explanation: The Tawa dam began to be built in 1958 and was completed in 1978. It submerged large areas of forest and agricultural land. In 1994, the government gave the rights for fishing in the Tawa reservoir to private contractors. The contractors began to threaten the villagers, who did not want to leave, by bringing in hoodlums. The villagers stood united and decided that it was time to set up an organisation and do something to protect their rights. They formed Tawa Matsya Sangh. In response to their protests, the government created a committee to assess the issue. In 1996, the Madhya Pradesh government decided to give the people displaced by the Tawa dam the fishing rights fortheir livlihoodin the reservoir.

questions.question: It as a living document___
questions.explanation: A constitution is a set of fundamental principles or established precedents according to which a state or other organization is governed. These rules together make up, constitute, what the entity is.This is the living entity of a democracy. When these principles are written down into a single document or set of legal documents, those documents may be said to embody a written constitution.

questions.question: A Dam is Built across a________sites, where one can collect a lot of water.
questions.explanation: A dam is a barrier that stops or restricts the flow of water or underground streams.Mainly the Dams are built across the rivers sites. Reservoirs created by dams not only suppress floods but also provide water for activities such as irrigation, human consumption, industrial use, aquaculture, and navigability. Hydropower is often used in conjunction with dams to generate electricity. A dam can also be used to collect water or for storage of water which can be evenly distributed between locations.

questions.question: Tawa is a
questions.explanation: The Tawa River is a tributary of the Narmada River of Central India. The Tawa is the Narmada's longest tributary, at 172 km. It rises in the Satpura Range of Betul and flowing north and west, joins the Narmada at the village of Bandra Bhan in Hoshangabad District. In 1958, construction began on Tawa Dam, which was completed in 1978 in Tawa Nagar to create Tawa Reservoir in southern Hoshangabad District.

questions.question: Indian constitution provides equality to___
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution recognises every person as equal. This means that every individual in the country, including male and female persons from all castes, religions, tribes, educational and economic backgrounds are recognised as equal. This is not to say that inequality ceases to exist. It doesn’t. But atleast, in democratic India, the principle of the equality of all persons is recognised. While earlier no law existed to protect people from discrimination and ill-treatment, now there are several that work to see that people are treated with dignity and as equals.

questions.question: Who was an African-American women who was an essential part of Civil Rights?
questions.explanation: African–Americans were treated extremely unequally in the United States and denied equality through law. For example, Rosa Parks was an African–American woman, Tired from a long day at work she refused to give up her seat on a bus to a white man on 1 December 1955. Her refusal that day started a huge agitation against the unequal ways in which African–Americans weretreated and which came to be known as the Civil Rights Movement. The Civil Rights Movement Rights Act of 1964 prohibited discrimination on the basis of race, religion or national origin. It also stated that all schools would be open to African–American children and that they would no longer have to attend separate schools specially set up for them.

questions.question: The key elements of a democratic government include following except
questions.explanation: The key elements of a democratic government include people’s participation, the resolution of conflict, and equality and justice. Equality is a key feature of democracy and influences all aspects of its functioning.

questions.question: Following provisions has been mentioned in our constitution except____________.

options.html: Every person has access to all public
No person can be discriminated against on the basis of their religion, race, caste, place of birth
Every person is equal before the law

questions.explanation: This recognition of equality includes some of the following provisions in the Constitution: first that every person is equal before the laws. Second, no person can be discriminated against on the basis of their religion, race, caste, place of birth or whether they are female or male. Third, every person has access to all public places including playgrounds, hotels, shops and markets. All persons can use publicly available wells, roads and bathing ghats. Fourth, untouchability has been abolished.

questions.question: The two ways in which the government has tried to implement the equality that is_______________.
questions.explanation: The two ways in which the government has tried to implement the equality that is guaranteed in the Constitution is first through laws and second through government programmes or schemes to help disadvantaged communities. There are several laws in India that protect every person’s right to be treated equally. In addition to laws, the government has also set up several schemes to improve the lives of communities and individuals who have been treated unequally for several centuries.

questions.explanation: Mid day meal scheme has helped reduce caste prejudices because both lower and upper caste children in the school eat this meal together, and in quite a few places, Dalit women have been employed to cook the meal.
