questions.question: In recent times goods are also sold through advanced means such as
questions.explanation: These days people do not have to go to the market to buy things they can buy products sitting at home through internet and home delivery. for e.g  sites like flipkart, amazon etc.

questions.question: Budh bazaar and Shani bazaar are the example of
questions.explanation: A weekly market is so called because it is held on a specific day of the week.Eg : Budh and Shanni bazar.

questions.question: Erodes merchants who sell goods to Delhi they sell to the foreigner who are from
questions.explanation: Delhi owners will use the cloth to make shirts. The shirts will be exported to foreign buyers. Among the foreign buyers are businesspersons from the US and Europe who run a chain of stores.

questions.question: The amount that is left or gained from earnings after deducting all the costs is called _______
questions.explanation: Profit: The amount that is left or gained from earnings after deducting all the costs.

questions.question: Who earn  the least in the Erode market
questions.explanation: Factories get the maximum work out of the weavers at the lowest possible wages.

questions.question: How much is the earning of Swapna from cotton cultivation

options.html: Earning from cotton cultivation more then with her earning if she would work as a labourer
Earning from cotton cultivation double with her earning if she would work as a labourer
Earning from cotton cultivation equals with her earning if she would work as a labourer
Earning from cotton cultivation less than with her earning if she would work as a labourer

questions.explanation: Swapna’s earning from cotton cultivation is barely more than what she might have earned as a wage labourer.

questions.question: What work do the women at the Impex garment factory produce
questions.explanation: In Impex garment factory women are employed as helpers for thread cutting, buttoning, ironing and packaging.

questions.question: Putting out system is prevalent in the ___ industry in most region of India
questions.explanation: The arrangement between the merchant and the weavers is an example of putting-out system, putting-out system,whereby the merchant supplies the raw material and receives the finished product. It is prevalent in the weaving industry in most regions of India.

questions.question: In which market goods are sold and purchase in large quantity
questions.explanation: A person who buys products in bulk from factory and may store them in godown, and sell it to other traders.

questions.question: A chain of market links the producer of cotton to the buyer at the ____
questions.explanation: Supermarket is series of markets that are connected like links in a chain because products pass from one market (producer) to another (buyer)

questions.question: These markets are not daily markets but are to be found at a particular place on one or maybe two days of the week. These markets most often sell everything that a household needs ranging from vegetables to clothes to utensils.
questions.explanation:
Weekly Markets :

1.It is held on a specific day of the week.

2.Most things are available at one place like vegetables, groceries household items, clothing items etc.People do not have to go to  different areas to buy different thing

questions.question: This is an enclosed shopping space. This is usually a large building with many floors that has shops, restaurants and, at times, even a cinema theatre. These shops most often sell branded products.
questions.explanation: There are other markets in the urban area which have many shops at one place called a shopping complex.  Multi-storeyed air conditioned buildings with shops on different floors known as malls.

questions.question: This refers to buying and selling in large quantities. Most products, including vegetables, fruits and flowers have such special markets
questions.explanation: In a wholesale trade products are bought in bulk from factory and may store them in godown, and sell it to other traders.

questions.question: A series of markets that are connected like links in a chain because products pass from one market to another.
questions.explanation:
A series of markets that are connected like links in a chain because products pass from one market to another. A typical chain of market is :

Producer - Wholeseller - Agents - Retailers - Buyers

questions.question: What does Weekly shop Traders do?

options.html: set up shops for the month and then close them up after month
set up shops for the week and then close them up after week
set up shops for the day and then close them up in the evening

questions.explanation: In a weekly market traders do not have permanent shops.They set up shops for the day and close them in the evening.

tests.name: Markets CBSE Online Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Social Science class 07 Social Science Markets CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Large multi-storeyed air-conditioned buildings with shops on different floors, known as
questions.explanation: There are other markets in the urban area which have many shops at one place called a shopping complex. Multi-storeyed air conditioned buildings with shops on different floors known as malls.

questions.question: Where can one-buy branded product from
questions.explanation: There are other markets in the urban area which have many shops at one place called a shopping complex.One can buy branded products from such complex.

questions.question: If you want to buy 100 pencil as return gift on your birthday where will you buy them from
questions.explanation: If one needs to purchase in bulk quantity then he /she should approach wholesaler. In the given case 100 pencils is to be bought hence one should go to the wholesaler.

questions.question: The arrangement between the merchant and the weavers is an example of which system?
questions.explanation: The arrangement between the merchant and the weavers is an example of putting-out system, putting-out system, whereby the merchant supplies the raw material and receives the finished product

questions.question: People assessed the market depends upon following factors except
questions.explanation: People assessed the market depends upon following factors except Advertisment

questions.question: Why are branded goods are preferred over non branded goods

options.html: They are sold in the mall

questions.explanation: Braded products are expensive and often promoted by advertising and claims of better quality.

questions.question: The Buying and selling takes place in ______ in the chain.
questions.explanation: A chain of markets links the producer of cotton to the buyer at the supermarket. Buying and selling takes place at every step in the chain.

questions.question: Swapna grows ___ on her small piece of land
questions.explanation: Swapna, a small farmer in Kurnool (Andhra Pradesh) grows cotton on her small piece of land

questions.question: Spinning mill buys the
questions.explanation: Spinning mill buys the bales and spins the cotton into yarn.

questions.question: ______ removes the seeds and presses the cotton into bales
questions.explanation:
Ginning mill buys the cotton then it removes
the seeds and presses the cotton into bales.

questions.question: Erode is a
questions.explanation: Erode is bi-weekly cloth market in Tamil Nadu

questions.question: Erode?s bi-weekly cloth market in Tamil Nadu is one of the largest cloth markets in the ___.
questions.explanation: Erode’s bi-weekly cloth market in Tamil Nadu is one of the largest cloth markets in the world. A large variety of cloth is sold in this market.

questions.question: ______ get the yarn from the merchant and supply him the cloth
questions.explanation: The merchant distributes work among the weavers based on the orders he has received for cloth. The weavers get the yarn from the merchant and supply him the cloth.

questions.question: Weavers invest all their savings or borrow money at high interest rates to buy looms. Each loom costs _______  so a small weaver with two looms has to invest Rs ______
questions.explanation: Weavers invest all their savings or borrow money at high interest rates to buy looms. Each loom costs Rs 20,000, so a small weaver with two looms has to invest Rs 40,000. The work on these looms cannot be done alone.

questions.question: Weaver?s _____ are one way to reduce the dependence on the merchant and to earn a higher income for the weavers.
questions.explanation: Weavers are paid very little by the.merchant under the putting out system. Weaver’s cooperatives are one way to reduce the dependence on the merchant and to earn a higher income for the weavers.

questions.question: The government buys cloth from the handloom weaver?s cooperatives and sells it through stores known as
questions.explanation:
The government procures the cloth for this programme from the powerloom weaver’s
cooperatives. Similarly, the government buys cloth from the handloom weaver’s cooperatives and sells it through stores known as Co-optex

questions.question: Those who purchase goods from abroad are known as
questions.explanation: Importer is a person or organization that brings goods or services into a country from abroad for sale

questions.question: It is a  market where goods are sold direct to the consumers
questions.explanation: Retail marketing is comprised of the activities related to selling products directly to consumers through various channels

questions.question: How Swapna is exploited by the traders

options.html: Swapna cotton fetches 6000 but traders pays her 3000
Swapna cotton fetches 8000 but traders pays her 3000
Swapna cotton fetches 2000 but traders pays her 3000
Swapna cotton fetches 1000 but traders pays her 3000

questions.explanation: At the trader’s yard weighing of the bags of cotton is done. At a price of Rs 1,500 per quintal, the cotton fetches Rs 6,000. The trader deducts Rs 3,000 for repayment of loan and interest and pays Swapna Rs 3,000.

questions.question: Which of the following is false regarding weekly market

options.html: Held on specific day of the week
Most of them are helped by their family members and, hence, do not need to hire workers.
Many things in weekly markets are available at costly rates
In weekly markets, these shop owners store the things they sell at home.

questions.explanation: Many things in weekly markets are available at cheap rates

questions.question: Shops in the neighbourhood are useful in many ways except

options.html: we can go there on any day of the week
Shops does not  provide goods on credit
Usually, the buyer and seller know each other

questions.explanation: Shops provides goods on credit

Women are employed at the lowest paid wages for the job
Workers wages are fived according to their skills

Arrange the process of making yarn from the cotton
(1) Sale of cotton
(2) Spinning Process
(3) Ginning Process
(4) Purchase of yarn by the dealer
questions.explanation:
1. Trader sells the cotton.
2.Ginning mill buys the cotton.Ginning mill removes the seeds and presses the cotton into bales.

3. Spinning mill buys the bales.Spinning
mill spins the cotton into yarn.
4. Spinning mill sells the yarn to yarn dealers.

questions.question: Identify what is shown in the figure below:-
questions.explanation: The given figure is of loom used in weaving.

questions.question: In the Impex garmet factory the highest paid among the workers are the tailors who get about Rs. _____ per month.
questions.explanation: In Impex garment factory ,yhe workers work on temporary basis. They can be asked to leave any  time. Their wages are fixed according to their skills. The highest paid among the workers are the tailors who get about Rs. 3,000  per month.

questions.question: Which of the following is false regarding weavers cooperatives

options.html: Weaver?s cooperatives are one way to increase the dependence on the merchant and to earn a higher income for the weavers.
They procure yarn from the yarn dealer and distribute it among the weavers.
In a weaver?s cooperative, the weavers form a group and take up certain activities collectively.
The cooperative also does the marketing.

questions.explanation: Weaver’s cooperatives are one way to reduce the dependence on the merchant and to earn a higher income for the weavers

questions.question: See what is shown in the figure below
questions.explanation: In the given image Women workers sewing buttons in a garment factory is shown.

questions.question:
Match the following
Column I
(a) Merchants
(b) Weavers
(c) Exporters
Column II
(1) Getting the woven cloth from the weavers
(2) Arrange cloth for selling to foreigners
(3) Spend long working hours
questions.explanation:
A.Merchants:
They purchase the yarn and  give instructions to the weavers about the kind of cloth that is to be made.
B.Weavers:
They make cloth and bring this to the Erode cloth market for sale. They also make cloth on order from the merchant.They spend long working hours.
C.Exporters:
 They use the cloth to make shirts to export them to foreign buyers.
